name: Decimals — Tenths, the Dot's First Secret
whatIsIt: A decimal is another way to write fractions whose pieces come in TENS: tenths, hundredths. The dot (decimal point) separates whole things from pieces: 2.7 means 2 wholes and 7 tenths — exactly the same as 2 and 7/10. Decimals are fractions wearing place-value clothes!
whyNeeded: The world runs on tens — our whole number system does! Money (₹2.50), height (1.4 metres), cricket run rates (7.5 per over), petrol (2.3 litres). Decimals let the place-value system you already know keep going PAST the ones place, into pieces.
realLifeUses: Measuring: your pencil is 12.5 cm — twelve and a half centimetres
Cricket: a run rate of 7.5 runs per over
Petrol pump display: 2.3 litres and counting!

vocabulary.meaning: The dot that separates wholes (left) from pieces (right): 2.7
One piece when a whole is cut into 10 equal pieces: 1/10 = 0.1
The first seat right of the dot — it counts tenths.

story.title: The Barfi Bar's Ten Pieces
story.text: The halwai cuts a long coconut barfi into 10 equal pieces. Meena buys 7 of them. 'How much of the whole bar is that?' she asks. 'Seven pieces out of ten — that is 7/10 as a fraction,' says the halwai. 'And with the dot we write it 0.7. The same amount, two names — the dot means a part of one whole.'
story.extractedProblem: 7 pieces of a barfi cut into 10 equal pieces — write it as a fraction AND a decimal.
story.answerInStory: 7/10 as a fraction, 0.7 as a decimal — the same amount with two names.

visual.caption: A strip cut into 10 equal parts: each part is one tenth = 1/10 = 0.1. Count the shaded tenths to read the decimal.

teachingGallery.title: Tenths — one whole cut into 10
teachingGallery.note: Each part is one-tenth = 0.1. The digit after the dot counts the tenths.

examples.caption: On the line, 9 tenths sits at 0.9.

standardMethod.summary: Count wholes (left of the dot) and tenths (first place right of the dot).
steps: Cut (or imagine) the whole in 10 equal pieces — each piece is a tenth.
Count complete wholes → write them LEFT of the dot.
Count leftover tenths → write them RIGHT of the dot.
2 wholes and 7 tenths → 2.7 (read: 'two point seven', meaning 2 and 7/10).

alternateMethods.name: Number-line method
alternateMethods.whenToUse: To see decimals as numbers with homes, just like fractions.
steps: Cut the space between 0 and 1 into 10 equal jumps.
Each jump is 0.1 — say them as you hop: 0.1, 0.2, 0.3…
0.7 lives seven hops from 0 — the same home as 7/10!
alternateMethods.example: A ruler is exactly this: the small marks between cm numbers are tenths.

alternateMethods.name: Fraction-translation method
alternateMethods.whenToUse: Whenever a decimal confuses you — translate it back!
steps: Read the tenths digit as a fraction over 10.
All your fraction skills work on decimals too.
alternateMethods.example: Compare 0.4 and 0.6 → 4/10 vs 6/10 → same bottoms, compare tops → 0.6 is bigger.

workedExamples.problem: A milk jug holds 1 litre, marked in 10 equal parts. Milk fills 1 whole jug and 3 marks of a second jug. Write the total as a decimal.
steps: Wholes: 1 full jug → 1 goes left of the dot.
Tenths: 3 marks → 3 goes right of the dot.

workedExamples.problem: Ravi says 0.9 is smaller than 0.10 'because 9 is smaller than 10.' Set the record straight (compare 0.9 with 0.1).
steps: So 0.9 is much BIGGER: nine tenths versus one tenth.
The digit right after the dot counts tenths — read it as a fraction and no trick can fool you.

trickPractice.trick: Tenths = one place after the dot
trickPractice.intro: How many tenths? That single digit goes right after the decimal point.

hintLadder: Tenths sit ONE place after the dot.
The top number is how many tenths.
Tenths sit ONE place after the dot.
The top number is how many tenths.
Tenths sit ONE place after the dot.
The top number is how many tenths.
Tenths sit ONE place after the dot.
The top number is how many tenths.
Tenths sit ONE place after the dot.
The top number is how many tenths.
Tenths sit ONE place after the dot.
The top number is how many tenths.

commonMistakes.mistake: Reading 0.7 as 'seven' or thinking 2.7 has 'twenty-seven' of something.
commonMistakes.fix: The dot changes everything after it into PIECES. 0.7 is seven TENTHS of one whole — less than 1! Translate: 0.7 = 7/10.
Compare place by place, just like whole numbers! Tenths first: 0.7 has 7 tenths, 0.25 has only 2 tenths — 0.7 wins. Longer decimals are NOT automatically bigger.

commonMistakes.mistake: Writing 'two and five tenths' as 25 or 2.05 instead of 2.5.
commonMistakes.fix: One seat per digit! Wholes LEFT of the dot, tenths in the FIRST seat right of the dot: two and five tenths = 2.5.

easy.q: A strip is cut into 10 equal parts and 3 parts are shaded. Write the shaded amount as a decimal.
hintLadder: Each part is one tenth.
3 tenths as a fraction is 3/10.
Zero wholes, 3 tenths: 0 point ?
The dot separates wholes from pieces.
The first seat after the dot counts what size of piece?
Tenths! 2 wholes + 7/10.

medium.q: Write 'one whole and 9 tenths' as a decimal.
hintLadder: Wholes go left of the dot: 1.
Tenths go in the first seat right of the dot: 9.
Put them together with the dot.
Compare the TENTHS seat first, like comparing from the left.
0.7 has 7 tenths; 0.25 has only 2 tenths.
7 tenths beats 2 tenths — the extra digit can't save it.

challenge.q: Translate the decimal 0.5 into its SIMPLEST fraction name. (Hint: it's an old friend!)
hintLadder: Simplify: divide top and bottom by the same number.

challenge.q: A pencil measures 12 cm and 5 small ruler marks more (each mark = one tenth of a cm). Write its length in cm as a decimal.
hintLadder: Wholes: 12 centimetres.
Pieces: 5 tenths of a centimetre.
Wholes, dot, tenths.
No wholes at all — what goes left of the dot?
Zero. Then the tenths seat.
0 point 8.
The digit after the dot counts tenths.
9 tenths.
Top 9, bottom 10.
Tenths seat first: 4 tenths vs 3 tenths.
4 > 3 — decided before the extra digit matters!
0.4 = 40/100 and 0.35 = 35/100 if you want proof.

questions.q: The jug shows 2 full litres and 3 tenth-marks. Write the amount as a decimal.
hintLadder: Wholes: 2.
Tenths: 3.
Wholes, dot, tenths.

teachBackPrompt: Explain the Dot Machine to someone at home: what lives left of the dot, what lives in the first seat right of the dot, and why 0.7 and 7/10 are the same amount. Use a ruler or a ₹ price as your example!

revisionCard.summary: Decimal = fraction in place-value clothes. Dot separates wholes (left) from tenths (right): 2.7 = 2 + 7/10. Each tenth = 1/10 = 0.1. Compare tenths first (0.7 > 0.25 — longer is NOT bigger). 0.5 = 1/2!

gameMission.title: The Dot Machine Delivery
gameMission.brief: The Dot Machine is stamping addresses for every piece in the Kitchen! Convert fractions to decimals and back, place them on the tenths line, and stop Ravi from declaring 0.25 the winner over 0.7.

realLifeProject: Ruler hunt: measure three small things at home (eraser, spoon, phone) to the nearest tenth of a centimetre. Write each length BOTH ways — decimal (4.5 cm) and fraction (4 and 5/10 cm).

rememberIt.hook: The dot splits whole from part. First place after = tenths
rememberIt.unpack: Places after the point: tenths, hundredths, thousandths (each one-tenth of the one before). 0.7 = seven tenths = 7/10.

studentQuestions.q: What does the first digit after the dot mean?
studentQuestions.a: Tenths - one of ten equal pieces of a whole. 0.3 means 3 tenths, 3/10.
Yes - five tenths (5/10) simplifies to one half.
